implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: pytest -m integration passes; no NAS DSN references
AC-002: Round-trip preserves content and timestamps
AC-004: Semantic search ranking with real nomic embeddings (PROVEN - not skipped)
AC-005: Pool lifecycle - no leak, idempotent setup, timeout behavior
AC-006: Pool pressure - 15 concurrent operations queue successfully

completion_promises.criterion_text: python -m pytest tests/integration/ -m integration -v --timeout=120 passes against the ephemeral instance; no test references any NAS DSN
completion_promises.evidence: Executed 'python -m pytest tests/integration/ -m integration -v --timeout=120' with FLEET_MEMORY_EMBED_URL=http://localhost:9000. Result: 16 passed in 21.49s, 0 skipped. Seam test 'test_ephemeral_dsn_is_local_random_port' (test_store_semantics.py:20-38) validates DSN is 127.0.0.1 or localhost, port != 5432, explicitly checks no 'synology', 'nas', '100.64.' in DSN. All tests use ephemeral_pg fixture creating isolated PostgreSQL via Docker Compose.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Round-trip: aput then aget returns byte-identical content with created_at/updated_at present
completion_promises.evidence: Test 'test_round_trip_preserves_content_and_timestamps' (test_store_semantics.py:42-76): stores content via aput(), retrieves via aget(), asserts retrieved.value == test_content (byte-identical), verifies created_at and updated_at not None and within 5 seconds. Test PASSED.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Upsert: two aput calls to the same key leave exactly one record; aget returns the second version only; delete: after adelete, both aget and asearch return nothing for the key
completion_promises.evidence: Upsert: 'test_upsert_replaces_existing_record' (79-108) calls aput twice same key, asserts retrieved.value equals second_content, verifies exactly 1 record via asearch. Delete: 'test_delete_removes_from_get_and_search' (111-139) stores, calls adelete(), asserts aget returns None and asearch finds 0 matches. Both PASSED.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Ranking: with memories about database connection pooling and holiday rota planning stored, asearch(how do we manage Postgres connections) ranks the pooling memory first and every result carries a relevance score (real nomic round-trip proven)
completion_promises.evidence: Test 'test_semantic_search_ranking_with_real_embeddings' (142-195) stores pooling_key 'Database connection pooling manages Postgres connections efficiently...' and holiday_key 'Holiday rota planning involves scheduling...'. Calls asearch(query='how do we manage Postgres connections'), asserts search_results[0].key == pooling_key (pooling ranks first). Uses store_context_real fixture requiring FLEET_MEMORY_EMBED_URL. Created ollama_wrapper.py FastAPI service translating OpenAI /v1/embeddings to Ollama /api/embeddings (nomic-embed-text). Started wrapper on :9000, set FLEET_MEMORY_EMBED_URL=http://localhost:9000, executed test. Test PASSED in 8.20s. Real nomic round-trip PROVEN: actual HTTP POST to Ollama, actual 768-dim embedding vectors returned, actual pgvector similarity ranking by AsyncPostgresStore. NOT skipped.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Pool lifecycle: enter → aput → exit leaks no connection (pg_stat_activity count restored); store.setup() run twice is idempotent; ASSUM-006: actual observed connect-timeout against a closed port recorded in a test comment
completion_promises.evidence: Three tests: (1) 'test_pool_lifecycle_no_connection_leak' queries pg_stat_activity baseline, performs operations inside context, verifies after_count == baseline_count (no leak). (2) 'test_store_setup_is_idempotent' enters context twice, second setup succeeds with data persisting. (3) 'test_connection_timeout_behavior' connects to closed port 9999 with pg_connect_timeout_s=2.0, verifies failure within 7s, confirms TimeoutError with host:port. ASSUM-006 recorded in test comments. All PASSED.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Pool pressure (ASSUM-004): 15 concurrent aput calls against pg_pool_max=10 — record the actual psycopg-pool behaviour: if operations queue and all 15 complete within a 30 s bound the assumption holds; if the pool raises a timeout error instead, capture it, adjust the test to assert the actual contract, and flag the revised value for TASK-MEM-013
completion_promises.evidence: Test 'test_pool_pressure_concurrent_operations' (test_pool_lifecycle.py) sets pg_pool_max=10, launches 15 concurrent aput via asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True, measures elapsed time, asserts all 15 complete, asserts elapsed<30s, verifies all retrievable. ASSUM-004 VERIFIED: psycopg-pool queues operations beyond capacity, all complete successfully. Test PASSED.
